signals.title: USD/NZD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Fed Outlook + RBNZ Divergence
signals.text: USD 1W avg +0.927% (n=7 pairs) vs NZD 1W avg -1.620% (n=4 pairs) — G10 spread 2.547% highlights strong USD outperformance and NZD underperformance. Fed hike bets are rising following Bessent Greenspan's 'tap the brakes' remark, signaling a potential Warsh hike in September, which supports USD strength. Conversely, NZD COT LF net short −22,454 (18.4% long) vs RBNZ market bias for a hike — a clear speculative short against a hawkish CB. This divergence, combined with extreme G10 weakness, elevates short-squeeze risk if RBNZ delivers. NZD/USD at 0.5636. Watch for RBNZ's next meeting on 8 Jul for confirmation of hike bias.

signals.title: USD/JPY — BoJ Hawkishness + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.70. BoJ's Tamura states Japan's neutral rate is about 2% and emphasizes getting closer sooner, signaling a hawkish shift. Despite this, the 264bp SOFR-TONA carry premium continues to favor USD, sustaining the bid. JPY COT LF net short −112,092 (29.7% long) indicates crowded speculative short positioning. MUFG notes intervention threat is slowing yen slide, but the BoJ hike failed to shift the USD/JPY trend. 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160.00. Watch for any further BoJ commentary or price action above 160.00.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Volatility + CESI
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6891. AUD COT LF net long +38,548 (70.2% long) indicates significant speculative long positioning. This is against a negative CESI for AUD (net -8 misses), suggesting data is disappointing expectations. The options market shows elevated hedging demand with AUD/USD IV 21.0% vs HV30 8.2%, resulting in a VRP of +12.8%. This combination of crowded long positioning, disappointing economic data, and high implied volatility suggests increased risk of long unwinds, especially if further data disappoints. Watch for AUD economic data releases for confirmation of negative bias.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Sentiment
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4233. The correlation between USD/CAD and WTI Oil has broken, with corr=-0.651 vs 252d norm=+0.031 (z=-2.18σ). This suggests the typical inverse relationship has inverted, with CAD strengthening despite oil weakness. Retail sentiment shows an extreme 95% short bias, indicating strong contrarian reversal risk if institutional flows continue to push against retail positioning. The Canadian Dollar has strengthened despite a hawkish Fed outlook, halting its recent winning streak. Watch for WTI Oil price action to see if the correlation normalizes or if the divergence persists.
